factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Escalation
factors.summary: Iran-Israel conflict approaching 100-day mark with active missile exchanges; US intercepted Iranian missiles over Gulf (6/6), Lebanon military losses reported. Dow has ~50% international exposure—elevated geopolitical tail risk compresses multiples.

factors.name: Sector Rotation Out of Cyclicals
factors.summary: Semiconductor rally reversed sharply Friday (VIX finally caught up per Bloomberg 6/6); credit strategists (DoubleLine, Oaktree) bracing for AI-driven credit bust. Dow overweight industrials/tech heavyweights vulnerable to earnings disappointment.

factors.name: Fed Deregulation Warning
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warned (6/6) that bank deregulation 'considerably weakens' supervision—risk-off signal amid financial boom. ECB preparing rate hike next week, tightening bias globally contradicts soft-landing narrative.

factors.name: Tariff & Trade Policy Uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump admin rolling out new forced-labor tariffs on 60 nations (6/6); Chinese EVs expected in US 'within years' despite restrictions. Dow multinationals (Boeing, CAT, GE) face margin pressure and supply-chain costs.

factors.name: Shipping & Input Cost Inflation
factors.summary: Container rates spiked 109% since Iran war started (Bloomberg 6/6); IATA director warns on jet fuel stagflation. Industrial input costs rising—headwind for Dow manufacturers (CAT, BA, MMM, HON).

factors.name: Earnings Sentiment Mixed
factors.summary: Strongest earnings season in years ending, but Wall Street macro soothsayers see different markets than stock analysts (Bloomberg 6/6). No major Dow component earnings catalyst visible in next 24-72h; lack of positive catalysts.

factors.name: China PBOC Gold Buying
factors.summary: China added to gold reserves in May (Bloomberg 6/6) amid bullion pressure—signals PBOC hedging demand but not a direct Dow driver. Neutral for equity risk sentiment.

factors.name: Consumer / Retail Weakness Signals
factors.summary: Luxury jewelry demand collapsing (MarketWatch 'no one wears bling' 6/6); retail sentiment fragility noted. Dow consumer names (HD, V, DIS) at risk if consumer confidence deteriorates further.

factors.name: Recent Price Action—Already Down 1.02% in 24h
factors.summary: Dow has already fallen 1.02% in last 24h and -0.58% in 4h. Move is underway; rally rejection from Friday's selloff intact. Suggests phase 2-3 extension of downtrend, not early-stage reversal.

factors.name: Banking Sector Vulnerability
factors.summary: GS (major Dow component) exposure to tightening, rate volatility, and credit stress. Fed deregulation warnings suggest complacency—risk of sudden repricing if financial conditions deteriorate.

screening.narrative: The Dow is in a PHASE 2 downtrend following Friday's avalanche selling that crushed equities off record highs. Underlying drivers are a toxic mix: geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel 100-day war, no ceasefire in sight), tariff/trade uncertainty, rising input costs (shipping +109% since war started, fuel stagflation from IATA), and a sharp reversal in semiconductor/AI enthusiasm (credit strategists hedging for bust scenario). Fed deregulation warnings and ECB rate hike primed for next week add to the hawkish global backdrop. Dow multinationals with heavy industrial and international exposure are particularly vulnerable to margin compression and FX headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: ECB interest rate decision (expected hike) within 7 days
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (weekly data typically Tuesday/Thursday)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (weekly/monthly)
Dow component earnings (Oracle next week mentioned; need full calendar)
China PMI release (follows typical mid-month schedule)
Cass Freight Index (real-economy bellwether)
CPI/PCE print (if within 2 weeks, major catalyst)
Trump-Netanyahu ceasefire negotiations (ongoing, highly uncertain)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran-Israel ceasefire agreement suddenly announced, (2) Fed signals dovish pivot (rate cuts expected in near term), (3) Treasury yields collapse sharply (>30 bps drop in 10Y), (4) Major Dow component reports blowout earnings and guides higher, or (5) Market finds technical support and reverses decisively above 51,100. Current setup is fragile—any short-term oversold bounce possible, but structural headwinds remain.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in phase 2; avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of resistance around 51,100–51,200, or for a multi-hour consolidation below 50,800 before re-entering bearish trades. Better entry on any failed bounce back to 51,000+. If support at 50,500 breaks cleanly, target 50,000 and monitor for panic liquidation.

screening.narrative: The Dow has retreated -1.02% over 24h after testing record highs earlier in the week, signaling a reversal from bullish euphoria into a Phase 2 consolidation. Macro backdrop is increasingly contested: escalating Iran-war tensions, imminent ECB rate hike, stagflationary energy cost pressures, and warnings of extended equity valuations have triggered profit-taking in mega-cap tech and semiconductors. Absent fresh hard economic data (ISM, CPI, durable goods), the bias defaults to neutral-to-cautious, though geopolitical and policy risks remain tilted downward. Prior SELL5/10 read (2026-06-07 03:42) has not yet triggered a clear breakdown; Friday's decline is modest and could be a retest rather than a trend reversal.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate hike announcement (this week, likely 2026-06-12 or earlier)
ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI (typically early-to-mid week)
Durable goods orders & factory orders (mid-week)
Oracle earnings & forward guidance (noted in news feed)
US–Iran escalation or ceasefire breakthrough (ongoing, high unpredictability)
Fed speakers & bank regulation comments (ongoing)
China PMI or GDP data (if released mid-week)
screening.risk_warning: If ECB hike is dovish-on-guidance or hawkish-on-growth, or if Iran war de-escalates sharply, risk-on appetite could reignite and push Dow back to 51,000+. Conversely, if geopolitical event (e.g., major strike, supply shock) or hard data miss (ISM < 48, durable goods -2%+) arrives, Dow breaks 50,500 support and accelerates lower into 50,000. Treasury yields and dollar index moves will be critical; no real-time data on those in feed limits precision.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway (PHASE 2) with modest 1% decline; avoid chasing shorts. Wait for a retest of 50,900–50,950 resistance or a break below 50,500 support to define next leg. If 50,500 holds, look for pullback entry into 50,650–50,750 on ECB/data timing clarity.

screening.narrative: The Dow has retreated 1% over 24h following a whirlwind week that saw record highs evaporate on Friday in an 'avalanche of selling.' Geopolitical tail risk (Iran war day 100, ongoing drone/missile exchanges in Persian Gulf) persists but appears contained; markets are pricing in a stalemate rather than escalation. Data-driven catalysts are absent from the news window—no ISM, no Fed signaling, no earnings—leaving the index vulnerable to rotation and profit-taking. ECB rate hike coming this week adds to global tightening pressure. The move down is underway but modest; this appears PHASE_2 consolidation rather than a breakdown.
catalysts_ahead: ECB interest rate decision (this week, 2026-06-06T20:00 context suggests imminent)
US ISM Manufacturing PMI (typically first Friday of month, next release ~June 6-13)

FOMC decision (if within 2 weeks, would cap confidence)
Iran escalation or ceasefire breakthrough (geopolitical binary risk)

screening.risk_warning: If major US inflation data (CPI/PCE) comes in hotter than expected, or if Iran conflict escalates militarily (direct attack on US bases, shipping disruption), Dow could face sharp downside. Conversely, if Fed signals dovish pivot or China announces stimulus, current weakness reverses quickly. The absence of high-conviction data in 24h window means conviction is low; a strong earnings beat (e.g., Oracle) or dovish Fed commentary could flip bias to BUY within hours.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase shorts; move is underway but lacks acceleration and data support. Wait for retest of 50,600–50,700 support or for fresh catalyst (ISM, FOMC, earnings) before committing. If support holds and earnings surprise to upside, watch for 51,000+ retest—no conviction trade in current environment.

However, the move is already underway with no fresh macro catalyst in the past 24h; earnings visibility remains thin, and the market is in a holding pattern ahead of ECB Thursday and US data later this week. Confidence in either direction is low pending those catalysts.

screening.narrative: The Dow has reversed sharply after a two-month rally, with Friday's selloff driven by record Nasdaq losses and crowded-trade unwinding fears. Geopolitical escalation (100 days into Iran war, Israeli strikes on Beirut) is lifting energy costs and weighing on airline/transport guidance. The ECB is poised to hike this week, adding to global rate-tightening pressure. However, the selloff is only -1% in 24h on the Dow itself—modest vs. broader market carnage—and AI mega-cap enthusiasm (SpaceX IPO, OpenAI pivot) offers some countercyclical bid. PHASE_2 condition: move is underway but not yet extended; heavy data calendar ahead (Mon-Tue) creates risk of whipsaw.

If Dow breaks below 50,500, lower support is 50,200. On the long side, wait for close above 51,000 + follow-through vol contraction before re-entering longs.

A -1.02% 24h decline and -0.58% in the last 4h suggest momentum is deteriorating, but the sell-off has already begun (PHASE_2), limiting fresh downside conviction.
